---
title: "NHANES Study 1 Dashboard: BMI & Income-to-Poverty Ratio"
author: "Mali Ocoko"
format:
  dashboard:
    theme: cosmo
    orientation: rows
    sidebar: true
    navbar:
      left:
        - text: "Study 2 Dashboard"
          href: study2_dashboard.qmd
date: last-modified
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
knitr:
  opts_chunk:
    comment: NA
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
set.seed(431)

required <- c("tidyverse","janitor","nhanesA","mice","broom","gt","plotly","DT","crosstalk","scales","patchwork","skimr","forcats")
to_install <- setdiff(required, rownames(installed.packages()))
if (length(to_install)) install.packages(to_install, repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse); library(janitor); library(nhanesA); library(mice)
  library(broom); library(gt); library(plotly); library(DT); library(crosstalk)
  library(scales); library(patchwork); library(skimr); library(forcats)
})
```

# Sidebar

```{r}
#| label: ingest
# NHANES ingest (2017–Mar 2020)
demo <- nhanes("P_DEMO") |> clean_names()
bmx  <- nhanes("P_BMX")  |> clean_names()
nh_all <- left_join(demo, bmx, by = "seqn")

# Fix types before deriving variables
nh_all <- nh_all |> mutate(
  riagendr = as.numeric(riagendr),
  dmdeduc2 = as.numeric(dmdeduc2),
  ridreth3 = as.numeric(ridreth3),
  indfmpir = as.numeric(indfmpir),
  bmxbmi   = as.numeric(bmxbmi),
  ridageyr = as.numeric(ridageyr),
  ridstatr = as.numeric(ridstatr)
)

# Clean variables (Study 1 uses complete cases only; no imputation)
nh <- nh_all |>
  transmute(
    seqn,
    ridstatr,
    ridageyr,
    sex = factor(riagendr, levels = c(1,2), labels = c("Male","Female")),
    race_eth = factor(ridreth3),
    education = case_when(
      dmdeduc2 %in% c(1, 2) ~ "Less than HS",
      dmdeduc2 == 3         ~ "HS Grad",
      dmdeduc2 == 4         ~ "Some College",
      dmdeduc2 == 5         ~ "College Grad",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    income_ratio = if_else(indfmpir > 5 | indfmpir < 0, NA_real_, indfmpir),
    bmi = if_else(bmxbmi > 80 | bmxbmi < 10, NA_real_, bmxbmi)
  )

# Study 1: complete cases on outcome + key predictor + covariates (strict complete-case)
study1 <- nh |>
  filter(!is.na(bmi), !is.na(income_ratio)) |>
  drop_na(ridageyr, sex, race_eth, education)

# SharedData for Crosstalk
sd1 <- SharedData$new(study1, key = ~seqn, group = "study1")
```

```{r}
#| label: filters
filter_select("sex1","Sex", sd1, ~sex, multiple = TRUE)
filter_select("race1","Race/Eth", sd1, ~race_eth, multiple = TRUE)
filter_select("edu1","Education", sd1, ~education, multiple = TRUE)
filter_slider("age1","Age", sd1, ~ridageyr, step = 1)
filter_slider("inc1","Income Ratio", sd1, ~income_ratio, step = 0.1)
filter_slider("bmi1","BMI", sd1, ~bmi, step = 0.5)
```

# KPI Cards

```{r}
#| label: kpis
kpi_df <- sd1$data(withSelection = TRUE) |>
  summarise(
    N = n(),
    Mean_BMI = mean(bmi, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD_BMI   = sd(bmi, na.rm = TRUE),
    Mean_Inc = mean(income_ratio, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
gt(kpi_df) |>
  fmt_number(columns = c(Mean_BMI, SD_BMI, Mean_Inc), decimals = 2) |>
  tab_header(title = "Current Selection — Summary (Study 1)")
```

# Distributions and Relationships

Row
--------------------------------

### BMI Histogram (Study 1)

```{r}
#| label: hist
p_hist <- ggplot(sd1, aes(x = bmi)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1) +
  labs(x = "BMI (kg/m^2)", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
ggplotly(p_hist, tooltip = c("x","y"))
```

### BMI vs Income Ratio (Study 1)

```{r}
#| label: scatter
p_scatter <- ggplot(sd1, aes(x = income_ratio, y = bmi,
                             text = paste0("seqn: ", seqn,
                                           "<br>BMI: ", round(bmi,1),
                                           "<br>Income ratio: ", round(income_ratio,2),
                                           "<br>Age: ", ridageyr,
                                           "<br>Sex: ", sex))) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0,5), breaks = seq(0,5,1)) +
  labs(x = "Income-to-Poverty Ratio", y = "BMI (kg/m^2)") +
  theme_minimal()
ggplotly(p_scatter, tooltip = "text")
```

# By-Group Views

Row
--------------------------------

### BMI by Education (Study 1)

```{r}
#| label: box
p_box <- ggplot(sd1, aes(x = education, y = bmi)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.2) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "BMI (kg/m^2)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  coord_flip()
ggplotly(p_box)
```

### BMI by Sex and Race/Eth (Study 1)

```{r}
#| label: vio
p_vio <- ggplot(sd1, aes(x = sex, y = bmi, fill = race_eth)) +
  geom_violin(draw_quantiles = c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75), alpha = 0.6) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "BMI (kg/m^2)", fill = "Race/Eth") +
  theme_minimal()
ggplotly(p_vio)
```

# Table

Row
--------------------------------

### Selected Records (Study 1)

```{r}
#| label: table
datatable(sd1, extensions = "Buttons",
          options = list(dom = "Bfrtip", buttons = c("copy","csv","excel","print"),
                         pageLength = 10, scrollX = TRUE))
```

# (Static) Model Snapshot

Row
--------------------------------

### Big vs Naive (Complete-Case, Training Split)

```{r}
#| label: models
set.seed(431)
training_sample <- study1 |> slice_sample(prop = 0.75)
test_sample     <- anti_join(study1, training_sample, by = "seqn")

mod_big   <- lm(bmi ~ income_ratio + ridageyr + sex + race_eth + education, data = training_sample)
mod_small <- lm(bmi ~ income_ratio, data = training_sample)

fits <- bind_rows(
  glance(mod_big)   |> mutate(model = "Big"),
  glance(mod_small) |> mutate(model = "Small")
) |>
  transmute(model, r2 = r.squared, adj_r2 = adj.r.squared, AIC, BIC, sigma)

gt(fits) |> fmt_number(columns = -model, decimals = 3) |>
  tab_header(title = "Model Fit (Study 1, Complete-Case)")
```

### Coefficients (Big Model, Study 1)

```{r}
#| label: coefs
gt(tidy(mod_big)) |> tab_header(title = "Big Model Coefficients (Study 1)")
```
